rationale: The observation's base_record_id corridor_hylan-boulevard-brt already matches the existing registry card for Hylan Boulevard in Staten Island. The registry card already includes the observation's source_id (2010_12_02_brt_hylan_open_house_slides) in its source_ids and the alias corridor_hylan-blvd-2010-study. No different registry record represents the same real-world corridor: corridor_stationary-staten-island is an aggregate borough-level camera index (streets list includes Hylan Boulevard), not an individual corridor — analogous to the do_not_merge_pair distinguishing corridor_archer-ave-busway from corridor_stationary-queens. Per the rule, when the closest match is the observation's own base_record_id, the decision is new.

evidence_refs.source_quote: A detailed map of the Hylan Boulevard Transportation Improvement Study Corridor. The corridor is highlighted with a thick red line, starting from the Verrazano-Narrows Bridge in Brooklyn, crossing the Narrows via Narrows Road and Steuben Street, and continuing through Staten Island along Hylan Boulevard.
A map of Staten Island, New York, showing the proposed Hylan Boulevard route. The route is highlighted with a thick red line and four green hatched rectangular boxes indicating initial study areas.
